public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as recruiter.
appearance: Sel Stone usually presents in layered civic fabric cut to show rank without inviting touch, with details that make their recruiter status legible before introductions begin.
manner: Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a recruiter still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of recruiter in and around Red Hood Guild, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Red Hood Guild workable through the obligations of recruiter while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
hidden_pressure: Must stay useful at the seam where policy becomes ordinary consequence. Around Red Hood Guild, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of recruiter visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Sel Stone can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Sel Stone can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. V33_1 additive depth pass preserved canon while strengthening at-table identity, appearance, and social motive.
public_signal: Sel Stone carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Red Hood Guild: the part of Red Hood Guild where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break

first_impression: Sel Stone presents as Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a recruiter still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Recruiter, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
what_they_refuse: Sel Stone refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Recruiter too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Sel Stone can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Sel Stone adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Sel Stone is Known locally through day-to-day work as recruiter. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Recruiter.
